themes.exampleQuote: One of the widest tea selections I have ever seen
a wonderful pseudo-'third space' where you can sip tea, relax, play a board game, study, and talk to other patrons
Staff is extremely friendly and knowledgeable about tea
It's hard to get a seat sometimes because it's crowded
The babaganoush is delicious, I went back just to try the food

notable_quote: One of my favorite places on Earth, a wonderfully large selection of teas from around the world, served in an intimate and quiet setting.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are consistent and effusive about the tea range, the bell-service ritual, and the room's ability to hold both solitary study and loud friend groups; the only recurring friction is seating scarcity during busy hours.
quarter_narrative: Dobrá Tea Pittsburgh arrived at Spring 2026 with the strongest review sentiment in the Pittsburgh Cafes family, 1,107 Google reviews at 82% five-star, a catalog of 690 photos spanning six tagged categories, and a conversion path (hours, phone, website, address) that is fully built out. What it didn't do was show up on Instagram: one post in 30 days, no Reels, and an engagement rate that sits well below the family median while Allegheny Coffee & Tea Exchange, this issue's café family leader, runs the table on social momentum. Dobrá holds the top tea-house rank on the strength of accumulated trust, but closing the gap to the café family's upper tier requires the Instagram signal to wake up.
tldr_read: Exceptional review sentiment and a complete conversion path, offset by a near-dormant Instagram, Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Tea Houses.
tldr_meaning: Every axis tied to on-platform reputation is at or above family median; the only ceiling on upward movement is an Instagram presence that posted once in the last month and has produced zero Reels.

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram with Reels, not photos
playbook.action: Zero Reels in 30 days against a family median engagement rate nearly 40% higher, short video of the table-bell ritual or a tea being prepared would close that gap faster than static posts.

playbook.headline: Invite creators into the ritual, not the menu
playbook.action: Creator Fit sits just below the family median, pitching the bell-service format and the board-game third-space angle gives visiting creators a specific visual hook rather than another café walkthrough.

playbook.headline: Tag food subcategories in the photo catalog
playbook.action: With 690 photos already uploaded, ensuring the baba ganoush, sesame cookies, and snack platters are tagged under distinct food subcategories deepens the visual catalog reviewers are already referencing.
